title: Module 1
content:
Introduction
to
accounting:
Cost
accounting,
Management
Accounting, Financial accounting. Scope, objectives and significance
of cost accounting, elements of cost, cost accounting standards, cost
accounting records, ledgers and cost statements.

title: Module 2
content:
Financial accounting: Salient features of balance sheet and profit and
loss statement, cash flow and fund flow analysis(elementary), working
capital management, ratio analysis - types, computation and
interpretation of financial ratios. Depreciation-definition and methods.

title: Module 3
content:
Cost accounting systems: Cost concepts relating to income
measurement and profit planning, cost concepts for control and
decision making, job costing, process costing, allocation of overheads,
activity based costing, standard costing and variance analysis, marginal
costing, break even analysis.

title: Module 4
content:
Budget and budgeting systems: Introduction,  planning process,
budget - definition, meaning and purpose, requirements for a sound
budget, fixed budgets - preparation of sales and production budgets,
financial budgets, flexible budgets, zero based budgets and budgetary
control, construction of various budgets.
